notes: Running the cpt_1.75_testbranch, this 2006 game has relatively poor framerates when compared to running the game on Windows due to DX9 to OpenGL translation.  Otherwise it is functional.  Radeon users can greatly improve performance with Gallium Nine and a forked Proton with Gallium Nine Extras.
title: Gothic® 3

cpu: AMD A4-4000 APU with Radeon HD Graphics
notes: The game works fine, except it has some font problems and every few minutes the game strangely lags then you need to pause and wait this brief lag spike to pass.
title: Gothic® 3

cpu: AMD A8-3870 APU with Radeon HD Graphics
notes: Performance could at times be a bit better. Also once in a while the game tends to crash. But I remember it from crashing on Windows every now and then as well. Hence gold.
title: Gothic® 3

notes: D9VK makes the game not render lightning and textures properly so I had to use WineD3D instead which makes the game stutter and not very smooth. Otherwise it's a playable experience.
title: Gothic® 3

notes: On Proton it works out of the box with WineD3D. If you use PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command% as a launch option (available since Proton 4.11) and I set in dxvk.conf (create it in the game folder) "d3d9.allowLockFlagReadonly = False" then the game works well with D9VK (D3D9 to Vulkan translation). Without this workaround the game is rendering mostly black textures. Tested with RADV and the new Mesa ACO compiler on Plasma Wayland.
title: Gothic® 3
